name: Castillo de Peñas Negras from Mora
name_original: Castillo de Peñas Negras desde Mora
region_intro: Hiking around Mora offers routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, reservoirs, and historic sites. The region features varied terrain, including paths near water bodies and trails leading to ancient fortifications. Hikers can explore areas with moderate elevation changes and open vistas. The trails provide opportunities to experience the natural and cultural heritage of Mora. Best hiking trails around Mora The most popular hiking route is Virgen de la Oliva Trail (PR-TO 22), a 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain with some elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castro Reservoir Trail, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) path. This trail offers views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Local hikers also love the Moracantá and Peña Manaera Trail, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Mora is defined by rolling hills, reservoirs, and historical points of interest like castles and crags. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Mora are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from 5 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Mora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Mora?
A: Yes, Mora offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021167294/castro-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.1-mile (5.0 km) path that provides pleasant views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas without significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Mora?
A: Hiking around Mora features varied terrain, including paths near water bodies like reservoirs and trails leading through rolling hills. You'll find routes with moderate elevation changes and open vistas, offering a mix of natural landscapes and historic sites.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Mora?
A: Many trails in Mora are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018783543/virgen-de-la-oliva-trail-pr-to-22" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Oliva Trail (PR-TO 22)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route covering 8.2 miles (13.2 km) with varied terrain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Mora?
A: Most hiking trails in Mora are dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Always check local regulations for specific trails, but generally, the natural environment is welcoming for pets.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in Mora?
A: Mora's hiking trails often pass by interesting historical and natural landmarks. You can explore areas near castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2127963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almonacid Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Orgaz&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021150409/castillo-de-penas-negras-from-mora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñas Negras from Mora&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, leads to a historic fortification. You might also encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3322095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finisterre Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Mora?
A: Yes, Mora has several trails suitable for families. The easier routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021167294/castro-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are generally well-suited for families with children due to their shorter distance and minimal elevation gain.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Mora?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Mora are typically spring and autumn, when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter offers a different charm, though some trails might be more challenging depending on conditions.

Q: How long do hikes around Mora typically take?
A: Hikes around Mora vary in length and duration. Easy paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021167294/castro-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 2 hours, while moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019452676/moracanta-and-pena-manaera-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moracantá and Peña Manaera Trail&lt;/a&gt; might take around 2 hours 38 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes can extend to 3-4 hours or more.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mora?
A: The trails in Mora are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied landscapes, the mix of natural beauty and historical points of interest, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Mora?
A: While many trails are easy to moderate, Mora does offer some more challenging options. There is at least one difficult route available for those seeking a more strenuous experience, often featuring greater elevation changes and longer distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197267541/crags-of-los-yebenes-trail-pr-to-15" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crags of Los Yébenes Trail (PR-TO 15)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with notable elevation, offering a good challenge.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Mora?
A: Information on specific public transport links directly to all trailheads in Mora is not extensively detailed. It is generally recommended to check local bus schedules or consider driving to access the starting points of many trails, especially those further from the town center. Some routes might be accessible from Mora itself.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Mora?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For many popular routes, designated parking areas are often available near the starting points, especially for trails that begin outside the immediate town area. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for parking recommendations for each individual hike.
tips: The top of the hill where the ruins of the castle of Peñas Negras are located is not only interesting to visit the ruins of the same but also offers beautiful panoramic views of the entire valley around the town of Mora where olive groves can be seen for kilometers and kilometers .

The Town Hall is perhaps the most unique building in the municipality, representing a beautiful metaphor with its name and use.
Its architect was Ezequiel Martín Martín, and its construction began in 1927 and was completed practically a year later in 1928. The tile-clad tower was completed in 1930.
